data.title: As a maintainer, I can publish a continuously-updated dev channel alongside the existing channel without affecting any current consumer
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Restructure .claude-plugin/marketplace.json from a single relative-path plugin entry into two git-subdir entries: dso and dso-dev. Both initially pin source.ref to main — no behavior change for existing consumers; opens the dev channel for early adopters.

**Why**: This is the foundation of the two-channel release model. Without two distinct plugin entries in marketplace.json, neither channel can be installed or auto-updated independently. Walking-skeleton story for Epic 36a7-dbb5.

**Scope**:
- IN: Convert single \"./plugins/dso\" relative-path entry into two git-subdir entries (dso and dso-dev), both pinning ref=main initially; ensure JSON validity post-edit (python3 -m json.tool or claude plugin validate); preserve marketplace name field.
- OUT: Cutting a release tag (Story 3); writing scripts/release.sh (Story 2); INSTALL.md updates (Story 2); CLAUDE.md / ADR updates (Story 4).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, marketplace.json exposes two plugin entries (dso and dso-dev), both using source.source=git-subdir with url=navapbc/digital-service-orchestra, path=plugins/dso, and ref=main.
  ← Satisfies: \"Clients can run /plugin install dso-dev@<marketplace-name> to subscribe to per-merge updates from main\"
- When this story is complete, a downstream client adding the marketplace and running /plugin install dso-dev@<marketplace-name> successfully installs the plugin from main.
  ← Satisfies: \"Clients can run /plugin install dso-dev@<marketplace-name> to subscribe to per-merge updates from main\"
- When this story is complete, clients who already have dso installed see no behavior change after refreshing the marketplace (dso continues to resolve to main HEAD until the cutover commit lands).
  ← Satisfies: \"After the cutover commit, a downstream client running /plugin marketplace update sees their installed dso plugin re-resolved to the latest release tag (not to main HEAD)\"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for marketplace.json structure validation (entries present, source types correct, refs correct).

## Considerations

- [Reliability] marketplace.json edits must produce valid JSON — validate post-edit (python3 -m json.tool or claude plugin validate).
- [Testing] Marketplace.json schema is fixed by Anthropic — assert against expected structure rather than against the current file content.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
